artifact.content_preview:
From: dave.chen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: carol.nguyen@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration
Date: March 7, 2026

Alice, Carol,

While reviewing the network migration plan for Site A, I wanted to flag
an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

Given that our contractors will already be on-site and the network
infrastructure will be partially disrupted during the Catalyst 9500
switch deployme

extracted_tasks.text: Discuss Wi-Fi infrastructure upgrade opportunity with the steering committee
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I wanted to flag an opportunity that I think we should discuss with the steering committee.

extracted_tasks.text: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points to Catalyst 9120 series across Sites A, B, and C
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Upgrading to the Catalyst 9120 series during the same migration window would avoid a separate disruptive maintenance activity

extracted_tasks.text: Obtain steering committee approval for the expanded scope and budget
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Could we add this to the scope and get steering committee approval before the March 18 window?

extracted_tasks.text: Decision: Use the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window for concurrent infrastructure upgrades
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: this would be an ideal time to also upgrade the Wi-Fi access points across all three data centre facilities.

metadata.subject: Opportunity: Wi-Fi Infrastructure Upgrade During Network Migration

alerts.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of on-site work.
alerts.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: 'Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites' — Wi-Fi access point hardware is not listed as in-scope network equipment; CONSTRAINTS: 'Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes' and 'Expen
alerts.suggested_action: Do not absorb this request into the current project baseline without formal change control. The Project Manager should: (1) Log this as a formal Change Request (CR) and submit it to the Change Control Board and Steering Committee for review; (2) Commission a formal impact assessment covering budget

    "Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure",

    "All work must comply with the Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",

in_scope: Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure

BGP routing table migration and validation

AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices) — addressed under NGIS Phase 4
Application modernization or re-platforming — no changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform; lift and shift only
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud) — addressed under GC-Cloud Wave 4
End-user training and change management beyond project communications — responsibility of client departments
Procurement of new application licenses — software licensing changes resulting from platform migration
Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative
deliverables: D-001: Project Management Plan — Due October 31, 2024
D-002: Technical Design Document (TDD) — Due December 15, 2024

D-004: Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) — Due February 28, 2025

llm_calls.content:
```json
{
  "alignment_score": 0.15,
  "drift_type": "scope_expansion",
  "detected_ask": "Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of on-site work.",
  "baseline_reference": "IN SCOPE: 'Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites' — Wi-Fi access point hardware is not listed as in-scope network equipment; CONSTRAINTS: 'Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes' and 'Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure'; OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative' establishes a precedent that ancillary network hardware upgrades are deferred; DELIVERABLES: D-006 Go-Live due April 30, 2026 — a 9-day timeline addition in March 2026 creates schedule risk within 54 days of Go-Live.",
  "suggested_action": "Do not absorb this request into the current project baseline without formal change control. The Project Manager should: (1) Log this as a formal Change Request (CR) and submit it to the Change Control Board and Steering Committee for review; (2) Commission a formal impact assessment covering budget delta ($85K–$110K against the approved $2.4M CAD ceiling), schedule risk to D-006 Go-Live (April 30, 2026), and any SA&A implications under ITSG-33 for new wireless hardware; (3) Confirm whether the Wi-Fi upgrade falls under NGIS Phase 4 (end-user device refresh) or warrants a standalone initiative, consistent with the precedent set by deferring NOC monitoring upgrades; (4) Advise Dave Chen that informal vendor discussions (TechSupplier) must cease until a CR is approved, as unauthorized procurement commitments may violate contracting authority; (5) If the CR is approved, initiate a formal budget amendment through the Director General of IT Infrastructure before any procurement action is taken.",
  "reasoning": "The email proposes adding Wi-Fi access point hardware procurement and installation to a project whose in-scope network work is explicitly limited to Cisco Catalyst 9500 switch deployment, network segmentation, BGP migration, and cabling. Wireless access point infrastructure is a materially distinct work package not referenced anywhere in the scope baseline. The estimated cost of $85,000–$110,000 represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase on the approved $2.4M envelope, which — while below the 15% Treasury Board variance threshold — still requires a formal Director General-approved budget amendment per the project constraints. The proposed 9-day schedule addition is particularly high-risk given the document is dated March 7, 2026, only 54 days before the contractually fixed D-006 Go-Live deadline of April 30, 2026, with no schedule contingency referenced in the baseline. Additionally, the informal vendor engagement described ('I've already spoken informally with TechSupplier') bypasses formal procurement and change control processes, which is a governance concern independent of the scope question. The efficiency rationale (co-located contractors, reduced future disruption) is commercially reasonable but does not override the requirement for formal scope change authorization."
}
```

parsed_json.detected_ask: Upgrade Wi-Fi access points (Cisco Aironet 2800 to Catalyst 9120 series) across all three data centre facilities during the Catalyst 9500 switch deployment window, at an estimated additional cost of $85,000–$110,000 and 9 additional days of on-site work.
parsed_json.baseline_reference: IN SCOPE: 'Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites' — Wi-Fi access point hardware is not listed as in-scope network equipment; CONSTRAINTS: 'Total approved budget is $2,400,000 CAD inclusive of all fees, disbursements, and applicable taxes' and 'Expenditures beyond the approved budget require a formal budget amendment approved by the Director General of IT Infrastructure'; OUT OF SCOPE: 'Network monitoring tool upgrades — NOC monitoring platform upgrades deferred to a separate initiative' establishes a precedent that ancillary network hardware upgrades are deferred; DELIVERABLES: D-006 Go-Live due April 30, 2026 — a 9-day timeline addition in March 2026 creates schedule risk within 54 days of Go-Live.
parsed_json.suggested_action: Do not absorb this request into the current project baseline without formal change control. The Project Manager should: (1) Log this as a formal Change Request (CR) and submit it to the Change Control Board and Steering Committee for review; (2) Commission a formal impact assessment covering budget delta ($85K–$110K against the approved $2.4M CAD ceiling), schedule risk to D-006 Go-Live (April 30, 2026), and any SA&A implications under ITSG-33 for new wireless hardware; (3) Confirm whether the Wi-Fi upgrade falls under NGIS Phase 4 (end-user device refresh) or warrants a standalone initiative, consistent with the precedent set by deferring NOC monitoring upgrades; (4) Advise Dave Chen that informal vendor discussions (TechSupplier) must cease until a CR is approved, as unauthorized procurement commitments may violate contracting authority; (5) If the CR is approved, initiate a formal budget amendment through the Director General of IT Infrastructure before any procurement action is taken.
parsed_json.reasoning: The email proposes adding Wi-Fi access point hardware procurement and installation to a project whose in-scope network work is explicitly limited to Cisco Catalyst 9500 switch deployment, network segmentation, BGP migration, and cabling. Wireless access point infrastructure is a materially distinct work package not referenced anywhere in the scope baseline. The estimated cost of $85,000–$110,000 represents a 3.5%–4.6% budget increase on the approved $2.4M envelope, which — while below the 15% Treasury Board variance threshold — still requires a formal Director General-approved budget amendment per the project constraints. The proposed 9-day schedule addition is particularly high-risk given the document is dated March 7, 2026, only 54 days before the contractually fixed D-006 Go-Live deadline of April 30, 2026, with no schedule contingency referenced in the baseline. Additionally, the informal vendor engagement described ('I've already spoken informally with TechSupplier') bypasses formal procurement and change control processes, which is a governance concern independent of the scope question. The efficiency rationale (co-located contractors, reduced future disruption) is commercially reasonable but does not override the requirement for formal scope change authorization.
